Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Orders Denying Access To Court: (1) Order Of 05-26-2016 Denying Access To Court By Interfering With Attorney-Client Relationship; And (2) Failure To Respond To Ex Parte Motion For Amendment Of 09-19-2014 Order For Entitlement To Estate Distribution; Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Yun Shang Zhou; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order.
Off Calendar Jul-29-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for Yung Shang Zhou (beneficiary).

The Court asked Mr. Bressler why he wishes to have another opportunity for additional argument. Mr. Bresler stated that the court is taking the wrong tact in regards to his client, that the Court has been more than reasonable in hearing all his arguments, that this motion was partially plead as a motion to vacate which is proper because there is no time limit on that and stated that this case shouldn't have gotten this far. Furthermore Mr. Bresler argued that the court was missing three waivers by foreign national in the court file and that the waivers are in contrary to the order signed by Judge Wiss.

In regards to the September 19, 2014 order, the Court asked why didn't Mr. Bresler file an appeal at that time. Mr. Bresler responded that he could have filed an appeal at anytime, but he believed that it was a simple clerical error that could be fixed at any time. In addition Mr. Bresler stated that he was told by a probate examiner that he could not change an order, defined a clerical error being distinguishable from judicial error by the fact that it does not involve careful consideration of the Court and reported that failure to give consideration to those waivers is a violation of a consular agreement. The Court stated that it understands Mr. Bresler's argument, that a characteristic of a clerical error is incorrect date, that what Mr. Bresler is suggesting is that the original trier of this case did not have critical information and wants the order to be reformed and suggested why not use the appeals process instead of saying he does not like the order so please relook at it and reform it.

Mr. Bresler responded with a question, "why didn't I do it then?" and stated "who cares" and that we are past the point of blaming somebody. Moreover Mr. Bresler reported that this was a simple clerical error that got blown up, that the Court doesn't have to reform the entire order at the same time, it can just do 1, 2 and 3, that the Court should not look at the results but at the appropriateness of the correction. The court restated that the information was in the file, that recommendations were given, an order was issued accordingly and that Mr. Bresler should have filed an appeal. Mr. Bresler argued that he is acting with in the guideline available to him and that the order can be vacated.

The Court stated that it is sympathetic to the amount of times that Mr. Bresler has appeared in Court to argue his position, that there is no finding that any documents were ignored, that the errors Mr. Bresler is suggesting is substantive and not clerical and asks what the other affected parties think of his analysis. Mr. Bresler stated that he only received feedback from his own client, that she said she would give away her share to take care of everyone and that he is making further effort to gets something for her. In addition Mr. Bresler stated that Court is affirming the waived rights of the foreign nationals. The Court stated for clarification that it is not going to undo and second guess what another judge has ruled on and that is what the Court of Appeals is for.

The Court took the motion for reconsideration under submission.

Judge Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Nancy Wong. Court Reporter: Nancy E. Presant-McDonald. CSR: 9906. Telephone Number: 818-551-7300. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Mrs. Yun Shang Zhous Petition For Amendment Of 9-19-2014 Order For Distribution, And To Vacate The 12-03-2015 Order; Memorandum In Support; Declaration Of Yun Shang Zhou; Request For Judicial Notice; Proposed Order
Off Calendar Apr-08-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Bresler for Yung Shang Zhou.

Mr. Bresler confirmed that they are the only interested party and that no one else is expected to appear. The Court asked counsel to explain why this is timely. Mr. Bresler stated that under 1008 it is not timely, that they hope to be forgiven, that they believed that the court would identify its error and correct it and provided information as to how the additional heirs were found. Mr. Bresler also stated that out of the six heirs, only one was actually due the benefit, that they cannot understand why the court will not fix the order, that the people benefitting from the court order are not a party to the case, that it could have been distributed to just the administrator, that it should have been distributed to either 1 person or all 6, but not to the 4. Mr. Bresler stated that his client had her own petition for entitlement which was ignored by the court and that they were never given the evidentiary hearing that they requested. The Court confirmed that the order was issued on 9/19/14, asked when was it decided that the order needed to be revised and asked why it was not dealt with earlier. Mr. Bresler replied that they were sensitive to the rules, that they understood this to be an oversight, that the court would not give the money to non-parties, that they thought that it was an error and filed an ex parte application to have it corrected. The Court stated that that was the wrong vehicle, that the remedy would have been to have sought reconsideration with the Judicial Officer who signed the order and asked why it should be viewed under the equal protection clause. Mr. Bresler stated that it should be considered because the court understands that out of the 6 only 1 is a party, that their request for an evidentiary hearing was not granted and that their petition was overlooked. The Court took the Motion for Reconsideration under submission.
